suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update kernel-auditor skill Step 5: explicitly state that verification tests (proving correctness, not just demonstrating bugs) are expected even when no bugs are found. Current wording says 'Write Failing Tests First (TDD) for EVERY code bug finding' and the 'Special Cases' section mentions adding SAFETY comments when no bugs are found, but doesn't mention verification tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: sync-primitives-audit worker added 22 verification tests when no bugs were found — correct behavior but not codified in the skill. Worker's own skillFeedback suggested this change.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance: 'In test files, prefer calling model methods over manually manipulating struct fields when the model provides a suitable method. Direct field manipulation creates fragile tests that may not track implementation changes.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: channel-handle-audit review found test close_wakes_peer_waiter (system/test/tests/channel.rs:330) manually replicating close_endpoint logic instead of calling the model method. Mitigated by another test but a preventable pattern.

rejectedObservations.observation: Handoff text says '10 channel + 11 handle' tests but actual counts are 11 channel + 10 handle (total 21 correct). Minor handoff documentation imprecision.
rejectedObservations.reason: Trivial documentation imprecision with no code impact. Total count is correct. Not worth action.

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature description mentions futex timeout paths that don't exist yet in the kernel.
rejectedObservations.reason: Known pre-existing limitation documented in AGENTS.md ('wait syscall timeout: Documented as not yet implemented. Out of audit scope'). Not a worker issue — feature description referenced future functionality.
